title: Mould-inhibiting spray
subtitle: A white vinegar, clove bud, and tea tree spray for bathroom grout, seals, and tiled surfaces
excerpt: White vinegar kills surface mould spores through acidity; clove bud and tea tree essential oils add antifungal activity to inhibit regrowth. Spray after every shower and leave to dry for ongoing prevention. For black mould that has penetrated grout, leave the spray on for ten minutes before wiping.
sourceNotes: Acetic acid activity against common surface mould: Nazzaro et al., Pharmaceuticals (2013). Clove bud EO antifungal properties: Bansod and Rai, Bioscience, Biotechnology and Biochemistry (2008). Tea tree antifungal activity: Carson et al., Clinical Microbiology Reviews (2006).

recipe.batchNotes: Scale proportionally. Keep each bottle to 250 ml so the essential oil concentration stays correct.
recipe.makeAheadNotes: Make fresh every two months; the essential oils oxidise over time and lose potency.
recipe.shelfLifeNotes: Use within two months. Discard if the spray loses its sharp clove and vinegar smell, which indicates the essential oils have oxidised.

glossaryTerms.definition: Steam-distilled oil from Syzygium aromaticum flower buds. Contains high levels of eugenol, which has documented antifungal activity. A skin sensitiser at concentrations above 0.5%; in a spray applied to surfaces and not to skin, this concentration is safe.

content.text: Surface mould on bathroom grout and silicone seals thrives in the warm, damp air left after showering. This spray combines the acidic action of white vinegar with
and tea tree, both of which have well-documented antifungal properties. Used daily after showering, it keeps bathroom surfaces in a hostile environment for mould spores.
Pour 200 ml of white vinegar and 50 ml of distilled water into the spray bottle. Add the essential oils. Swirl to combine. Label with the date.

For prevention: shake well and spray over grout lines, silicone seals, and the shower tray after use. Leave on the surface -- do not rinse. The vinegar and oils work as they dry. Ventilate the bathroom well to help it dry quickly.

For existing surface mould: spray liberally onto the affected grout and leave for 10 minutes, then scrub with an old toothbrush and wipe off. Repeat on stubborn patches. For black mould that has penetrated deep into grout, a commercial chlorine bleach product is more effective -- this spray works best as a regular preventative rather than a one-off cure.

Safe on ceramic tiles, glass shower screens, plastic tray and enclosures, and standard silicone seals. Do not use on natural stone (marble, travertine, limestone) -- the acidity of the vinegar etches these surfaces. Not for use on chrome or aluminium fittings where prolonged acid contact can cause discolouration.
Keep at room temperature, out of direct sunlight. Use within two months. The spray has lost its effectiveness when the sharp clove and vinegar smell is no longer detectable.
